name: CaMV 35S promoter
notes: Strong constitutive promoter widely used in dicot transformation. Not signal-responsive — use as positive control.

name: Nopaline synthase promoter
notes: Weak constitutive promoter. Common in selection cassettes.

name: AtNRT2.1 promoter
notes: Nitrate-inducible promoter from the high-affinity nitrate transporter NRT2.1. Induced under low nitrate, repressed under high nitrogen.

name: RD29A promoter
notes: Stress-inducible promoter responsive to drought, cold, and ABA. Contains DRE and ABRE cis-elements.

name: Metallothionein 2A promoter
notes: Induced by copper, zinc, and cadmium. Metal-responsive elements (MREs) in promoter region.

name: PHT1;1 promoter
notes: Strongly induced under phosphate starvation. Root-preferential expression.

name: AtSUC2 promoter
notes: Companion cell-specific promoter. Useful for phloem-loading constructs.

name: Enhanced Green Fluorescent Protein
notes: Fluorescence reporter. Requires UV/blue light excitation. Non-destructive imaging. May require plant codon optimization.

name: Beta-glucuronidase
notes: Histochemical staining reporter. Destructive assay (X-Gluc). High sensitivity. Gold standard for promoter characterization in plants.

name: Betanin biosynthesis cassette (CYP76AD1, DODA1, cDOPA5GT)
notes: Visible color reporter producing red-violet pigment. Non-destructive. Detectable by eye without equipment. Ideal for field biosensors.

name: Firefly luciferase
notes: Luminescence reporter. Requires luciferin substrate. Quantitative with luminometer. Good for real-time kinetics.

name: Anthocyanin biosynthesis (PAP1/MYB75 transcription factor)
notes: Visible purple pigment reporter. Non-destructive. Uses endogenous plant pathway. Color visible in vegetative tissues.

name: NOS terminator
notes: Most widely used terminator in plant transformation. Reliable transcription termination and polyadenylation.

name: CaMV 35S terminator
notes: Strong terminator from CaMV. Often paired with 35S promoter.

name: Octopine synthase terminator
notes: Alternative Agrobacterium-derived terminator. Used when tNOS is already in the construct to avoid recombination.

name: HSP18.2 terminator
notes: Strong plant-derived terminator. Higher expression than tNOS in some contexts.

name: TMV Omega leader sequence
notes: 5' UTR translational enhancer. Increases protein expression 5-10x when placed between promoter and coding sequence.

name: Plant Kozak consensus sequence
notes: Optimized translation initiation context for plants (AACAATGGC). Include around start codon for efficient translation.
